discovery.text: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The great Prussian boulevard — lined with linden trees as the name promises, connecting the Stadtschloss to the Brandenburg Gate.

narrative.summary: The great Prussian boulevard — lined with linden trees as the name promises, connecting the Stadtschloss to the Brandenburg Gate.

blocks.title: Why Jacobs Chose It
blocks.body: Wide central promenade, grand institutional buildings (Humboldt University, the Arsenal, the opera), linden trees in double rows, the Brandenburger Tor anchoring the west end. One of the few great streets that survived the 20th century more or less intact.

blocks.title: How to Walk It
blocks.body: Walk the full length. Jacobs is explicit: you have to walk a great street to understand it — the sequence, the proportions, how the edges change. Give it an hour minimum. Sit at a café or on a bench midway. Notice what makes it feel alive.
